patients.name: Sarah Jenkins
patients.symptoms: Mild shortness of breath, generalized fatigue, slight cough post-bacterial pneumonia recovery.
patients.labResults: WBC: 11.2 K/uL (slightly elevated), Hb: 12.1 g/dL, Creatinine: 0.9 mg/dL. Chest X-Ray shows residual infiltrates in lower left lobe.
patients.medicalHistory: Type 2 Diabetes (managed with Metformin), Hypertension (Lisinopril 10mg daily), admitted last month for acute bacterial pneumonia.
patients.aiRiskAnalysis: Patient displays medium readmission risk due to age (64), comorbid hypertension and type 2 diabetes, and lingering post-pneumonia residual infiltrates. Shortness of breath indicates slow respiratory recovery. Recommended to monitor oxygen saturation twice daily, schedule a pulmonary follow-up within 7 days, and strictly regulate blood glucose levels.

patients.name: Michael Chang
patients.symptoms: Severe acute chest pain radiating to left shoulder, fever, persistent dry cough, tachypnea, severe breathing difficulty.
patients.labResults: Troponin I: 0.05 ng/mL (borderline), CRP: 42 mg/L (high), WBC: 14.5 K/uL (elevated sepsis marker). Blood cultures pending. D-Dimer: 850 ng/mL.
patients.medicalHistory: Hypercholesterolemia (Atorvastatin 20mg), mild asthma, no previous cardiac surgeries.
patients.aiRiskAnalysis: HIGH RISK: Patient is in sepsis-prone inflammatory status with SpO2 of 91% on room air. Elevated CRP and WBC combined with tachypnea and fever meet SIRS criteria. Risk of escalation to severe acute respiratory distress syndrome (ARDS) or acute coronary event is elevated. Immediate oxygen therapy, broad-spectrum IV antibiotics, and urgent echocardiogram/CT pulmonary angiogram are recommended.

patients.name: Elena Rodriguez
patients.symptoms: Localized swelling, warmth, and throbbing pain in lower left abdomen. Nausea but no active emesis.
patients.labResults: WBC: 9.8 K/uL (normal range), Lipase: 35 U/L (normal), Urine hCG: Negative.
patients.medicalHistory: Ovarian cysts removed via laparoscopy in 2024. Penicillin allergy.
patients.aiRiskAnalysis: Low readmission risk. Vitals are completely hemodynamically stable. Normal WBC and negative pregnancy test rule out major acute pelvic infection or ectopic pregnancy. Abdominal ultrasound is planned to evaluate for localized ovarian cyst or focal hernia. Outpatient management remains highly probable after diagnosis.

patients.name: Marcus Vance
patients.symptoms: Post-operative knee stiffness, mild incisional pain, mild localized erythema, no joint effusion.
patients.labResults: Hb: 11.5 g/dL (stable baseline), Platelets: 240 K/uL, INR: 2.3 (therapeutic on Warfarin).
patients.medicalHistory: Total Left Knee Arthroplasty (TKA) performed on June 24. Chronic Atrial Fibrillation (Warfarin therapy), Mild Osteoarthritis.
patients.aiRiskAnalysis: Moderate risk primarily tied to age (78) and anticoagulant management (Warfarin) for atrial fibrillation. Left knee incision is clean and intact. Physical therapy compliance is excellent. Risk of deep vein thrombosis (DVT) is mitigated by ongoing Warfarin therapy, but requires careful INR tracking to prevent hematoma or excessive incisional bleeding.

appointments.notes: Post-pneumonia clinical evaluation. Run spirometry test if SpO2 maintains > 95%.
Follow-up regarding cyst sonogram results and blood panels.

items.description: ICU Intensive Care Unit Bed (1 Day)
